Milorad Dodik
During three decades in politics Milorad Dodik has morphed froma liberal anti-nationalist and darling of Western powers to acommitted supporter of independence for Republika Srbska, theSerb- majority entity within Bosnia and Herzegovina (BiH)established after the 1995 war. Having served as the semi-autonomous territory’s prime minister twice between 1998 and2010 and its president twice between 2010 and 2025, he is now afree-floating political agitator. He is an ally to both Serbia andRussia and a thorn in the side of the BiH government and itsWestern guarantors. The underlying ethnic and sectariandivisions that have long destabilised the Balkans will smoulder in2026. Mr Dodik could set them aflame once again.
